message.content:
# http-proxy-middleware

## Overview

`http-proxy-middleware` is a widely-used Node.js package that provides HTTP proxy middleware, commonly used with Express, Connect, and other compatible frameworks. It wraps the `http-proxy` library (and in the upcoming major version, `httpxy`) to simplify proxying requests in Node.js applications.

As of the week of April 11–17, 2026, the package sees approximately **24.1 million weekly downloads** on npm, reflecting its extensive adoption across the Node.js ecosystem — it is a core dependency of tools like `webpack-dev-server` and `create-react-app`.

## Known Vulnerabilities

The package has three publicly disclosed vulnerabilities tracked in the OSV/GitHub Advisory database:

### CVE-2024-21536 — Denial of Service via micromatch (GHSA-c7qv-q95q-8v27)

- **Severity:** Denial of Service (DoS)
- **Affected versions:** All versions before 2.0.7; versions 3.0.0 to before 3.0.3
- **Fixed in:** 2.0.7, 3.0.3
- **Summary:** An `UnhandledPromiseRejection` error thrown by the `micromatch` dependency when processing certain request paths could crash the Node.js process. An attacker could exploit this by sending crafted requests to kill the server.
- **References:**
  - [NVD entry](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2024-21536)
  - [Snyk advisory](https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-JS-HTTPPROXYMIDDLEWARE-8229906)
  - [Fix commit (v2.x)](https://github.com/chimurai/http-proxy-middleware/commit/0b4274e8cc9e9a2c5a06f35fbf456ccfcebc55a5)
  - [Fix commit (v3.x)](https://github.com/chimurai/http-proxy-middleware/commit/788b21e4aff38332d6319557d4a5b1b13b1f9a22)

### CVE-2025-32996 — Double writeBody call (GHSA-4www-5p9h-95mh)

- **Affected versions:** 1.3.0 to before 2.0.8; 3.0.0 to before 3.0.4
- **Fixed in:** 2.0.8, 3.0.4
- **Summary:** A logic error in `fixRequestBody` where an `if` / `if` pattern was used instead of `if` / `else if` could cause `writeBody` to be called twice on the same request, leading to unexpected behavior or errors.
- **References:**
  - [NVD entry](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-32996)
  - [Fix PR #1089](https://github.com/chimurai/http-proxy-middleware/pull/1089)
  - [Fix commit](https://github.com/chimurai/http-proxy-middleware/commit/020976044d113fc0bcbbaf995e91d05e2829a145)

### CVE-2025-32997 — fixRequestBody proceeds after bodyParser failure (GHSA-9gqv-wp59-fq42)

- **Affected versions:** 1.3.0 to before 2.0.9; 3.0.0 to before 3.0.5
- **Fixed in:** 2.0.9, 3.0.5
- **Summary:** The `fixRequestBody` utility did not check whether the body parser had failed before proceeding, potentially forwarding malformed or invalid request bodies to the upstream target.
- **References:**
  - [NVD entry](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-32997)
  - [Fix PR #1096](https://github.com/chimurai/http-proxy-middleware/pull/1096)
  - [Fix commit](https://github.com/chimurai/http-proxy-middleware/commit/1bdccbeec243850f1d2bb50ea0ff2151e725d67e)

## Recommended Minimum Versions

Based on the public advisories, users should be on at least:

| Branch | Minimum Safe Version | Resolves |
|--------|---------------------|----------|
| 2.x    | **2.0.9**           | All three CVEs |
| 3.x    | **3.0.5**           | All three CVEs |

Versions prior to 1.3.0 are only affected by CVE-2024-21536 (the micromatch DoS). Users on any version below 2.0.7 should upgrade.
